message.content:
@startuml
actor SalesClerk
participant "«presentation»\nDefineNewCategoryUI" as DefineNewCategoryUI #D3E5F2
participant "«application»\nDefineNewCategoryController" as DefineNewCategoryController #B0A8FF
participant "«builder»\nCategoryBuilder" as CategoryBuilder #97DECE
entity "category : «domain»\nCategory" as category #FFE6CC
database "«repository»\nCategoryRepository\n  \t\t   " as repository #FFFFFF

SalesClerk ->> DefineNewCategoryUI:1 Define new category 
activate DefineNewCategoryUI 

DefineNewCategoryUI -->> DefineNewCategoryController:2 create  
defineNewCategoryController -[#black]-> defineNewCategoryController  

alt asks user information   
    salesclerk <--#dashed-- definewnewcategoryui:4 Code , Description    
else addCategory(categoryCode, categoryDescription)     
     definenewcategorycontroller -->> categorybuilder:6 create      
      activate categorybuilder       
       categorybuilder <-[hidden]- category        
        loop for each field in fields          
            if hasField(field.name)==true               
                call setFieldValue(objectToBuildInstance.fieldName,valueOfTheCurrentIteration);                
                 else                 
                  throw exception("field not found");                  
               endif            
           endloop         
         deactivate categorybuilder           
          return objecttoBuildinstance;             
endif    

salesclerk <<--[red]definewnewcategoryui:#green displayinfo() ;return true/false based on success/failure of operation performed by controller method invoked earlier via message number three above i.e., 'add' or similar depending upon context here we are adding so let's say yes/no accordingly whether added successfully otherwise failed due some reason like duplicate entry etc.. then show appropriate msg back again using this same line but changing color codes appropriately according to situation e.g red=error green=succesful blue=inprogress yellow=pending/waiting response from server side processing request sent through previous step(s). Also note there may be multiple such lines required per use case scenario being modeled out visually within sequence diagrams themselves which could involve more than one round trip between client/server components interacting together collaboratively towards achieving desired outcome/goal/objective/task at hand! So keep track carefully while drawing these kinds complex scenarios involving many steps/messages exchanged among various actors/entities involved throughout entire process flow depicted graphically
